title: Enforce MCP role authorization with per-delegation capability tokens
source: OWASP LLM Top 10 (2025) — LLM06 Excessive Agency / broken authorization; codebase gap: mcp_server.py trusts CTO_AGENT_ROLE env var with no verification
description: mcp_server.py derives the caller identity purely from the CTO_AGENT_ROLE env var (used in team messages, file reservations) with no verification. Any subprocess can spoof its role to send team messages, steal file reservations, or invoke tools it shouldn't. Bind each delegated agent to a short-lived capability token issued at spawn time and verify it inside the MCP tools that mutate shared state.
proposed_changes: In delegate.py, when spawning a Morty, generate an HMAC capability token = HMAC(session_secret, role|ticket_id|nonce) and pass it via env CTO_AGENT_TOKEN alongside CTO_AGENT_ROLE. Add verify_agent_token(role, ticket_id, token) to security_utils.py. In mcp_server.py, wrap state-mutating tools (send_team_message, reserve_files, update_ticket_status, close_ticket) with a decorator that verifies the token and rejects (audit_log_security_event 'mcp_authz_fail') on mismatch. Keep read-only tools open to avoid breaking observability.
